newsSince: No new material company-specific news since June 9. Q4/FY26 results (record FY revenue ₹2,153.7cr +11.3%, but Q4 PAT -50.5% YoY to ₹47.9cr on one-off provisions/input costs, prompting a ~19-20% share drop and a 1000%/₹100 dividend) were announced May 5-6 — well before the June 9 cutoff. The ₹1,200cr backlog, April ₹310cr/2,107 MVA orders, and a new power-transformer facility commissioning in July 2026 are pre-existing. Shares up ~5.7% since June 9.

sources.label: Voltamp Q4 FY26 results, board approved May 5 (ScanX)
Voltamp FY26 record revenue ₹2,153.69cr (Power Peak Digest)

verdicts.rankRationale: Scaling electricity/transformer-bottleneck lens: Q4 PAT miss (-50.5%) and ~19% drop were May 5-6, pre-cutoff, not new. Record FY26 revenue, ₹1,200cr backlog, July facility ramp keep the bottleneck thesis intact. No new news since Jun 9. Hold #1.
verdicts.bull: Record FY26 revenue, ₹1,200cr backlog, fresh April orders (₹310cr/2,107MVA), and a new power-transformer plant (Jul 2026) extend the India transformer up-cycle.
verdicts.neutral: Top-line strong but Q4 margins hit by one-off provisions/input costs; India electricals already re-rated.
verdicts.bear: Q4 PAT halved; rising copper/input costs and rich valuation after re-rating leave little margin of safety.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: July 2026 power-transformer facility commissioning and backlog conversion at better margins.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Input-cost squeeze and order/competition normalization compressing the re-rated multiple.
